title: Cloud Save Implementation
description: Comprehensive guide for implementing cloud save functionality with conflict resolution, offline support, and data synchronization across devices. Covers a three-tier architecture with local persistence, sync engine, and remote storage backend.
intents: data synchronization between local and remote
optimistic local writes with background sync
anti_patterns: synchronous-only save that blocks the UI thread
no conflict resolution strategy for concurrent offline edits
trust client state as authoritative without server validation
transmitting full save payloads instead of deltas
ignoring offline scenarios and network failures
using last-write-wins without pluggable merge functions for structured data
no retry or backoff strategy for failed sync operations
storing unversioned save data without device identifiers
no integrity verification (checksums) for save data in transit
implementation_steps: Design the three-tier architecture: local persistence layer (IndexedDB for browser, SQLite for native), sync engine (bidirectional data flow, conflict detection/resolution), and remote storage backend (distributed key-value store with strong consistency)

Implement the SaveEntry data model with userId, saveKey (namespace for multiple save slots), data (JSON payload), version (monotonic counter), deviceId (origin device), timestamp (server-assigned ISO8601), and checksum (SHA256 integrity hash)

Build the version tracking system: increment version atomically on the server using compare-and-swap operations, tag each save entity with version number and device identifier to reconstruct chronological order of edits across offline devices
Implement conflict detection: server compares incoming version against stored version; if incoming version is not exactly one greater than stored version, flag a conflict
Implement automatic merge for structured data types: list appends merged by interleaving entries by timestamp, map updates merged field-by-field with type-specific strategies
Build manual conflict resolution UI: when automatic merge is not possible, present both versions to the user for selection or manual merge, then upload resolved version with new version number

Implement offline support with durable outbox: queue all write operations locally, serve reads from local store during offline periods, use Network Information API and service worker lifecycle events to detect connectivity changes and trigger sync

Implement error handling with exponential backoff: start at 1 second, cap at 5 minutes, add jitter to prevent thundering herd; after 10 consecutive failures enter degraded mode (serve local data, notify user); retry transient errors (timeout, 503) automatically; trigger re-authentication for permanent errors (401, 403)

Optimize performance with delta encoding (transmit only changed fields, up to 80% bandwidth reduction), gzip compression with 1 KB threshold, batching of operations within 5-second windows, and prefetching on app launch using access frequency heuristics
rules: Optimistic local writes with background sync: never block the UI on network operations
Per-entity version vectors for conflict detection: each save entry must have a monotonically increasing version number and device identifier
Server-assigned timestamps: the server is the source of truth for temporal ordering, not client clocks
Checksum verification on both upload and download to detect data corruption in transit
Atomic version increments using compare-and-swap on the server to prevent lost updates
Three-step conflict resolution: detection (version mismatch) → automatic merge (type-specific strategies) → manual resolution (user choice)
Durable outbox pattern for offline writes: all writes persist locally before being synced
Exponential backoff with jitter for retry: 1s initial, 5 min cap, prevent thundering herd
After 10 consecutive sync failures, enter degraded mode: serve local data and notify user of cloud sync unavailability
Delta encoding for bandwidth optimization: only transmit changed fields, not full payloads
Compression threshold: apply gzip only to payloads larger than 1 KB to avoid overhead on small saves
Batch multiple save operations within a 5-second window into a single upload request
No direct trust of client state: server validates all incoming data before persisting

examples.name: SaveEntry Data Model
examples.description: A save entry with userId, saveKey ('profile', 'gameState', 'settings'), JSON data payload, monotonic version counter, device identifier, server-assigned ISO8601 timestamp, and SHA256 checksum for integrity verification

examples.name: Offline Sync Flow
examples.description: User edits save data while offline → writes queued in durable outbox → network restored → outbox flushed to server in order → conflict detected if version mismatch → automatic or manual resolution

examples.name: Conflict Resolution - Automatic Merge
examples.description: Two devices modify the same save key offline; on sync, list appends are interleaved by timestamp, map updates are merged field-by-field without user intervention

examples.name: Error Handling - Degraded Mode
examples.description: After 10 consecutive sync failures, system continues serving local data with user notification that cloud sync is unavailable; transient errors retried with backoff, permanent errors trigger re-auth flow
